title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense · Language Clinic: Rollback Cache
summary: The newest artifact replaces production without a route diff, removed URLs are discovered by readers, rollback requires rebuilding, caches preserve broken assets, ownership ends after deployment, and generated volume is called completion. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rollback cache increment in production publication, migration, and reco…
objectives: Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.
Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_release_recovery_defense_3(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Rollback Cache evidence · Theory Rollback Cache
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-rollback-cache-1i0294" that uses case 4320 to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Rollback Cache evidence · Theory Rollback Cache.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rollback Cache into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Rollback Cache evidence · Theory Rollback Cache, keep Rollback Cache connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-rollback-cache-1i0294
# Competency: ssg-rollback-cache.
# Case 4320: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_rollback_cache_1i0294(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1i0294 = "1i0294-4920i1-4320"
    assert evidence_variant_1i0294
    evidence_axes_1i0294 = (
        "reader1i0294",
        "source4320",
        "parse4920i1",
        "route1i02944320",
        "artifact43201i0294",
        "recovery4920i14320",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1i0294)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"

Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Write the theory evidence note for Rollback Cache: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 3513 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Rollback Cache model · Theory Rollback Cache.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-rollback-cache-1i0294.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-rollback-cache-1i0294(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_rollback_cache_1i0294\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?accounted_routes != routes_after)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not recovery_owner)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
